name: mc_regime_match_quality_typical_regime
description: Typical-regime negative fixture. The historical candidate pool is wide (60 days of SPY returns spanning a normal-amplitude band) and today's query point sits IN THE MIDDLE of the candidate-pool joint distribution on both kNN features (return, rolling vol). Under z-score standardization the squared Mahalanobis-style distance from today to the K nearest candidate-pool days is bounded by the chi-squared 0.99-quantile (df=2) ~= 9.21. compute_regime_match_quality MUST report is_unprecedented=False; the existing MC veto stays in effect and the trailing-stop exit gate remains gated by the MC sanity threshold. Telemetry row records the score and the suppression decision (False).
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history. Today's query is constructed to sit at the candidate-pool centroid in both features (spy_today_return = pool mean, today_vol approx pool mean vol); under z-scoring the squared distance approaches zero on both features so mean^2 distance is bounded well below chi2(2)_{0.99}. Fixture asserts the inequality, not a specific producer-computed score.
derivation: 60-day candidate pool with SPY returns following a sinusoidal sweep amp*sin(2*pi*i/7) around +0.001 (period 7 days) -- a deterministic varied pattern that gives the rolling-vol feature a non-degenerate spread across the candidate window (z-score std > 0 on BOTH features, unlike a perfectly-alternating sequence whose rolling vol collapses to a constant). Today's spy_today_return=0.1 pct (=0.001 decimal) equals the pool mean on the return feature; today_vol is computed from the pool tail + today and sits within the candidate-pool vol distribution. Post-z-score query coordinates land near the candidate-pool centroid (|today_ret_z|, |today_vol_z| both bounded), so the mean squared Euclidean distance to the K=8 nearest candidates is well below chi2(2)_{0.99} ~= 9.21. Fixture asserts is_unprecedented=False.
